role: Co-founder of Combatant Clergy Association; Council of Islamic Revolution chairman
bio: Morteza Motahhari was one of the most influential clerical scholars of the Islamic Revolution. A devoted student of [Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini) in Qom, he co-founded the Hussainiyyah Ershad and the [Combatant Clergy Association](/orgs/combatant-clergy-association) (JRM). He served as chairman of Khomeini's Council of the Islamic Revolution in early 1979. He was assassinated on 1 May 1979 by the [Forqan Group](/orgs/forqan-group). His intellectual legacy underpins much of the Islamic Republic's ideological framework. He is the father-in-law of former [Majlis](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly) Speaker [Ali Larijani](/people/ali-larijani) and the father of MP [Ali Motahari](/people/ali-motahari).
editorial_notes: Assassinated 1 May 1979. Father-in-law of Ali Larijani (who married his daughter Farideh). Father of Ali Motahari (MP). 12 Ordibehesht is now Teachers' Day in Iran in his honour. Birth year sometimes cited as 1920.
